headline: INDIA HALTS GOLD AND SILVER IMPORTS — 13 TONNES STRANDED AT CUSTOMS AS GOVERNMENT CLEARANCE LAPSES
snippet: Indian banks halted all gold and silver import orders after a DGFT authorisation expired March 31 without renewal, stranding 5+ tonnes of gold and 8+ tonnes of silver at customs. India is the world's second-largest gold consumer and the largest silver buyer globally — the halt risks a supply shock heading into the peak Akshaya Tritiya buying season.

headline: GAGAN GUPTA BACKS CORA GOLD'S MALI MINE WITH $120M STREAM — SHARES UP 13%
snippet: Eagle Eye Asset Holdings — backed by Indian tycoon Gagan Gupta — will fully fund Cora Gold's Sanankoro project in southern Mali via a $120 million gold stream, taking 30.44% of production at 20% of spot. The deal removes all remaining project financing risk and pushed Cora Gold shares up 13% on announcement day.

headline: GOLD HITS $4,887 — FOURTH STRAIGHT WEEKLY GAIN ON US-IRAN CEASEFIRE TALKS
snippet: Spot gold rose 1.7% to $4,887/oz on Friday — its highest since March 17 — as optimism over US-Iran ceasefire negotiations reduced inflation-risk fears. Gold closed the week up 0.8%, marking four consecutive weekly gains, and has gained roughly 15.6% since January 1. Up next: Q1 earnings season for major gold producers.

headline: SILVER CHARGES TOWARD $80 — UP 143% YEAR-OVER-YEAR AS GOLD-SILVER RATIO CONTRACTS FROM 105
snippet: Silver hit $79.60/oz Friday, approaching one-month highs, driven by US-Iran diplomacy and sustained industrial demand. The metal is up 5.65% over the past month and 143% year-over-year. The gold-silver ratio's compression from above 105 is a historically bullish signal — when this ratio last contracted from these levels, silver outperformed gold by over 30% in the following six months.

headline: USA RARE EARTH (USAR) SURGES 7% AS PHASE 1A OKLAHOMA MAGNET LINE COMMISSIONED
snippet: USA Rare Earth stock jumped 7.11% Friday after commissioning Phase 1a of its NdFeB sintered magnet line in Stillwater, Oklahoma — the first domestic US mine-to-magnet rare earth operation. The company guides to 600 metric tonnes/year of magnet capacity by end-2026, scaling to 1,200 MT by early 2027, with Arnold Magnetic Technologies as primary distribution partner.

headline: MOTLEY FOOL: WHY I WOULDN'T TOUCH USA RARE EARTH IN THE CRITICAL MINERALS ARMS RACE
snippet: A contrarian take on Friday's USAR surge: the analyst argues USA Rare Earth remains pre-revenue and is scaling from scratch with significant execution risk, with real profitability unlikely before 2028 even with the defense-mandate tailwind. The mine-to-magnet narrative is compelling — but so is the cash burn rate at current production ramp targets.

headline: AMERICAS GOLD & SILVER POSTS RECORD Q1: 787,000 OZ SILVER, UP 76% YEAR-OVER-YEAR
snippet: Americas Gold & Silver (TSX: USA | NYSE-A: USAS) reported record Q1 2026 consolidated silver production of 787,000 oz — up 76% from Q1 2025 — with record silver sales of 830,000 oz. The Idaho Galena Complex drove the surge, setting up the company's record 64,000-metre 2026 drill program targeting further resource expansion.

headline: THE DISCOVERY PREMIUM: WHY COPPER-GOLD POLYMETALLICS ARE COMMANDING HIGHER VALUATIONS IN 2026
snippet: Analysis finds copper-gold polymetallic discovery assets commanding a valuation premium as producers chase multi-revenue deposits with shared capital structure. A projected 300,000-tonne copper concentrate shortfall, copper targeting $12,000+/tonne, and demand from AI infrastructure and grid expansion are converging to re-rate junior explorers with copper-gold drill intercepts.

headline: URANIUM SPOT ABOVE $100/LB — AI DATA CENTER POWER DEMAND ENTERS THE SUPPLY EQUATION
snippet: Spot U3O8 has traded above $100/lb since late January 2026, with Sprott's weekly watch noting AI data center power demand has added a structural demand pillar alongside utility offtake. Supply response remains limited: Denison's Phoenix ISR project (under construction in Saskatchewan) and Paladin's Langer Heinrich mine (targeting full production by June) represent the key near-term additions.

headline: GOLDSEEK COT REPORT: GOLD, SILVER AND USDX SPEC POSITIONING FOR WEEK ENDING APRIL 17
snippet: The weekly Commitments of Traders report for gold, silver, and the US Dollar Index shows spec positioning entering a potential fifth consecutive week of gold gains. Large speculator net longs have risen in step with price, while silver's commercial short unwinding is consistent with continued momentum. USD index positioning reflects growing risk appetite on ceasefire diplomacy.
